metadata_information.title: A RMSM-X (Revised Minimum Standard Model) for Chile
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: A RMSM-X (Revised Minimum Standard Model) for Chile

document_description.abstract: The RMSM-X model for Chile is one of a sequence of models that also includes, in increasing order of complexity, the RMSM-XX and MACOR models. The three models share the same budget accounts for an economy disaggregated into several sectors - such as private, public, financial, and foreign - and organized in a flow of funds framework. The RMSM-X model combines a simple behavioral structure with the basic accounting framework and can be solved recursively to obtain macroeconomically consistent projections for a set of endogenous variables. RMSM-XX will more completely specify the links among economic variables and will require a simultaneous solution procedure. MACOR will be a standard medium size macroeconomietric model that will introduce a more sophisticated behavioral structure into the basic accounting framework. The author also describes two possible solution procedures or closure rules for the model. They are the "normative" closure and the "positive" closure.
